name: Laguna de la Estanca and Embalse del Perdiguero Loop from Calahorra
name_original: Laguna de la Estanca y Embalse del Perdiguero – circular desde Calahorra
description: This moderate 22.9-mile touring cycling route from Calahorra explores Laguna de la Estanca and Embalse del Perdiguero.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Calahorra traverse the Ebro River basin, characterized by agricultural landscapes and the Cidacos River valley. The terrain generally features gentle undulations, with some routes incorporating moderate climbs towards reservoirs or historical sites. The region offers a network of paths, including dedicated greenways, suitable for various cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Calahorra The most popular touring cycling route is Laguna de la Estanca and Embalse del Perdiguero Loop from Calahorra, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route explores local reservoirs and natural areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cidacos Greenway – Cidacos Greenway loop from Calahorra, a moderate 37.1 miles (59.7 km) path. It follows the dedicated Cidacos Greenway, offering a smooth ride through the valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Cidacos Greenway – Castle of Arnedo loop from Calahorra, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through the Cidacos River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Calahorra is defined by river valleys, agricultural plains, and historical towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy touring bike routes Calahorra to more advanced touring bike trails Calahorra. The routes in Calahorra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Calahorra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Calahorra?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Calahorra, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Calahorra?
A: Yes, Calahorra offers several easy touring cycling routes. The terrain generally features gentle undulations, especially along the Ebro River basin and Cidacos River valley. You can find routes suitable for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevations for touring routes in Calahorra?
A: Distances for touring routes in Calahorra vary, with many popular options ranging from 20 to 40 miles (30 to 60 km). Elevations are generally moderate, often featuring gentle climbs towards reservoirs or historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994439093/laguna-de-la-estanca-and-embalse-del-perdiguero-loop-from-calahorra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Estanca and Embalse del Perdiguero Loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; is 22.9 miles with about 385 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Calahorra?
A: Yes, for more experienced touring cyclists, there are routes with greater distances and elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14940431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra – El Perdiguero Reservoir loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.6 km route with over 680 meters of ascent, offering a more demanding ride.

Q: Do Calahorra's touring routes include dedicated cycle lanes or greenways?
A: Yes, a significant feature of touring cycling in Calahorra is the inclusion of dedicated greenways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4274184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a smooth and pleasant ride through the valley. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3981352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Cidacos Greenway loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; utilize this excellent infrastructure.

Q: Are there many circular touring cycling routes around Calahorra?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Calahorra are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. This includes popular options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Castle of Arnedo loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic circular journey.

Q: What natural and historical sights can I explore along Calahorra's touring routes?
A: Calahorra's touring routes offer a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Perdiguero Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5259467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, or discover unique natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Autol and its Picuezos&lt;/a&gt;. The routes often traverse agricultural plains and river valleys, providing diverse scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Calahorra?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Calahorra is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cooler with a chance of rain.

Q: What are the touring cycling conditions like in Calahorra during winter?
A: During winter, touring cycling conditions in Calahorra can be cooler, with a higher chance of rain. While many routes remain accessible, it's advisable to check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out. The agricultural landscapes may also appear less vibrant than in warmer months.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Calahorra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Calahorra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes river valleys and agricultural plains, and the well-maintained greenways that make for enjoyable rides.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Calahorra?
A: Yes, many of the routes, particularly those utilizing the dedicated greenways like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4274184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, are suitable for families. Their generally flat and well-surfaced paths provide a safe and enjoyable environment for cyclists of all ages.
tips: Spectacular rock monoliths, one approximately 45 meters high and the other about 30 meters, located in Autol. A real gem of nature that is definitely worth a visit!

Fortress built in the year 1470 formed by a rectangular keep and three heights.

The castle stands on a hill about 100 meters high, with privileged views of the surroundings, where it was possible to monitor and communicate with other fortresses.

It is intuited that, at first, the space was a Roman settlement that only had a watchtower, but that later, and during the Muslim domination, the castle was built.
Excellent place to eat and spend a full day of cultural visits. A labyrinth of streets and humble houses as used by the Romans and Jews who lived here for more than five centuries.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for the Laguna de la Estanca y Embalse del Perdiguero circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated cycling time for this route is just over three hours, making it a manageable ride for touring cyclists with good fitness levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, which are ideal for touring bikes. However, be aware that some segments classified as 'moderate' might include unpaved sections. While the paths generally have a good firm surface, a section passing under a national road and highway could be less well-maintained.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience varied scenery, including pine forests within Mount Perdiguero, views of dry cultivated fields, and a complete view over the Embalse del Perdiguero (Perdiguero Reservoir) and the Los Agudos mountains. Don't miss the View of El Picuezo and La Picueza, the historic Castillo de Quel, and the charming Calahorra Old Town.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the Camino Natural Via Verde del rio Cidacos, the Camino Jacobeo del Ebro, and the Vía Verde del Cidacos.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de la Estanca y Embalse del Perdiguero circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires a good fitness level due to its length of nearly 37 kilometers and an elevation gain of around 385 meters. While mostly paved, the potential for unpaved sections means it's not entirely an easy ride, so it might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route from Calahorra?
faqs.answer: Since this is a circular route starting and ending in Calahorra, you can typically find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas in Calahorra, especially those convenient to the route's starting point, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de la Estanca y Embalse del Perdiguero route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is popular with walkers and runners, and dogs are generally welcome on trails in this region, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when cycling, for their safety and the safety of other trail users. Be mindful of any specific local regulations or signage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. These seasons also provide beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Laguna de la Estanca y Embalse del Perdiguero circular route. It is freely accessible for public use.

faqs.question: Are there any alternative routes or shorter options in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter option, there's a 4.79 km circular route specifically around Perdiguero. While designed for running, it's also suitable for hiking and primarily uses trails through pine forests, alongside some road sections and dry crops, offering a different experience.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the fire watchtower on Mount Perdiguero?
faqs.answer: From the fire watchtower located on the summit of Mount Perdiguero, you can expect particularly picturesque and complete views over the Embalse del Perdiguero (Perdiguero Reservoir) and the surrounding Los Agudos mountains, offering a fantastic panoramic perspective of the Rioja Baja region.

waypoints.name: View of El Picuezo and La Picueza
